tasks.userTest: Run npm run dev, visit each route and verify skeleton loaders appear during initial paint. Force an error on one route, verify the error.tsx renders. Run npm test, npm run lint, npm run build, npm run typecheck, and npx playwright test — all must pass.

reviewFindings.issue: Uses `h-90 w-90` on line 21 which are NOT valid Tailwind CSS classes. The default Tailwind spacing scale jumps from 80 (20rem) to 96 (24rem) — there is no 90. The build passes because Tailwind only strips unused classes from CSS output, not from HTML attributes. The skeleton image placeholder collapses to 0×0 and is invisible, so the loading skeleton does NOT visually match the About page layout (which uses a 360×360 image). Should use `h-[360px] w-[360px]` or `h-96 w-96`.
Line 13: The span skeleton placeholder has `text-2xl` class that is dead code — spans don't render text content and this class has no visual effect. Harmless but should be removed for cleanliness.

Implementation notes state error pages should include `revalidate` to allow retry on next request, but all error pages only use `reset` via `({ reset }: { reset: () => void })`. In Next.js App Router, `reset` provides equivalent retry functionality (attempts to rerender the segment), so this is not a functional issue — the implementation notes were aspirational rather than a hard requirement.

The task claims 'all 8 Jest snapshot tests pass' and 'all 9 E2E tests pass', but the actual test suite has 39 Jest tests (10 suites, 15 snapshots) and 20+ E2E tests across 9 spec files. The acceptance criterion counts are outdated; the actual test coverage is more comprehensive than stated.
